project_type: Python Streamlit web application - multi-page data analysis tool
build_system: pip with requirements.txt, deployable to Streamlit Community Cloud
test_framework: Manual testing via streamlit run; syntax validation via python -m py_compile
test_command: python -m py_compile app.py && python -m py_compile pages/1_Community_College_Dashboard.py && python -m py_compile pages/2_Upload_and_Analyze.py && python -m py_compile pages/3_AI_Insights_Engine.py && python -m py_compile pages/4_Advanced_Analytics.py && python -m py_compile utils/data_generator.py && python -m py_compile utils/ai_insights.py && python -m py_compile utils/visualizations.py && python -c "import app"
verification_instructions: 1. Run pip install -r requirements.txt to install dependencies. 2. Run py_compile on all Python files to verify syntax. 3. Run 'python -c "import app"' to verify imports work. 4. Optionally run 'streamlit run app.py' to verify the app starts.
snapshot_or_generated_files: data/community_college_data.csv - synthetic dataset generated by utils/data_generator.py
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - all packages can be installed. Python 3.9 available. Streamlit Community Cloud deployment target.
contribution_requirements: Code should be clean, well-documented, production-quality. App must work with or without OpenAI API key (graceful fallback to rule-based insights).
key_patterns: Multi-page Streamlit app using pages/ directory convention. Utility modules in utils/ directory. Session state for cross-page data sharing. Plotly for interactive visualizations. OpenAI integration with fallback.
directory_structure: app.py (main entry), pages/ (multi-page app pages), utils/ (shared utilities), data/ (datasets), .streamlit/ (config)
